summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns at various temperatures (350 °C, 325 °C, 300 °C, 275 °C, and 250 °C) showing peaks corresponding to specific crystal planes ( (111), (200), and (220) ).

a.question: What type of diffractogram is shown in the figure?
a.answer: The grazing incidence X-ray diffraction (GIXRD) of the deposited films has been studied.

a.question: How does the reflection of the LiF crystal planes change as the temperature increases?
a.answer: The same LiF crystal planes ( (111), (200), and (220)) can be observed in the figure, with a slightly different intensity for the (220) plane at different temperatures.

a.question: At which temperature does the (200) plane exhibit the highest intensity?
a.answer: As the temperature increases, intensity of the (200) plane increases too. The highest intensity is observed at 350 °C.

a.question: In what way does the temperature affect the presence of an impurity phase?
a.answer: No impurity phase has been observed for any of the temperatures studied.
